experiments.title: Exact scaling exponent table
experiments.hypothesis: The recorded velocity, vorticity, pressure, and force exponents follow from one exact scaling rule.
invariants: Space dimension equals 3
Parabolic time power equals 2
experiments.certificate: Unit-test assertions are the algebraic certificate.
experiments.success_criterion: All canonical critical and energy exponents equal their exact rational targets.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The dossier or implementation has a scaling error.

experiments.title: 2607 logarithmic exponent bookkeeping
experiments.hypothesis: The paper's stated distribution and rearrangement exponents transform algebraically as claimed.
invariants: This checks exponent arithmetic only
It does not prove O'Neil or energy inequalities
experiments.success_criterion: Vorticity tail (3/2,3/2) maps to rearrangement (2/3,1) and velocity tail (3,3).
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one claimed exponent conversion is algebraically inconsistent.

experiments.title: Pinned arXiv 2607.08866v2 source cache
experiments.hypothesis: The exact TeX source can be reproducibly fetched, hashed, and safely extracted.
invariants: Version v2
No tracked copy without redistribution permission
No unsafe archive paths
experiments.method: navier_lab.source_cache fetches the arXiv e-print and writes a manifest.
experiments.certificate: SHA-256 manifest plus archive member list.
experiments.success_criterion: The manifest names chaos_sphere.tex and records a nonempty SHA-256 digest.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The source version is not pinned or the archive layout changed.

experiments.title: Source-anchored audit of 2607 foundation obligations
experiments.hypothesis: The exact v2 source permits a bounded verdict on O2607-01 through O2607-04 without assuming any downstream commutator estimate.
invariants: Pinned TeX SHA-256
Every source display and imported theorem is line-anchored
No downstream theorem conclusion is assumed
experiments.method: Parse the complete TeX source, rewrite all foundation quantifiers, track constant dependencies, and independently derive the tensor cancellation.
experiments.certificate: The proof map and its linked analytic counterexamples and tensor derivation.
experiments.success_criterion: Each of O2607-01 through O2607-04 receives a verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict with exact scope.
experiments.failure_interpretation: At least one foundation quantifier, dependency, or tensor identity remains unresolved.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) multi-core localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound alone does not confine every high-level set to one ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: Divergence-free realization is not needed for the first measure-theoretic counterexample
A later PDE-consistent version is separate
experiments.method: Construct separated critical spikes with controlled distribution function and unbounded covering diameter.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and executable distribution/covering check.
experiments.success_criterion: The example has bounded weak-L(3/2) quasi-norm while violating single-ball containment uniformly.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The proposed spike weights or geometry do not preserve the critical bound.

experiments.title: Weak-L(3/2) one-core anisotropic localization counterexample
experiments.hypothesis: A uniform weak-L(3/2) bound does not force even one connected point-centered high-level set into a ball of radius proportional to lambda^(-1/2).
invariants: The distribution exponent remains exactly 3/2
The example is scalar and makes no PDE-consistency claim
experiments.method: Use anisotropic boxes with half-width powers 1/4, 5/8, and 5/8.
experiments.certificate: A written proof and exact rational/integer exponent checks.
experiments.success_criterion: The scaled distribution tail is constant while the necessary critical-radius covering constant grows as lambda^(1/4).
experiments.failure_interpretation: The anisotropic volume or diameter exponents were calculated incorrectly.

experiments.title: Logarithmic covering entropy in the truncated-energy step
experiments.hypothesis: A countable high-vorticity cover replaces single-ball localization whenever its aggregated local weak-L(3/2) gain vanishes uniformly, and the resulting ell-(3/2) ledger is sharp from the local bounds alone.
invariants: The localized commutator constant is uniform in centre, radius, and time
Weak-L(3/2) distribution functions aggregate with exponent 3/2
A measurable disjoint refinement removes overlap multiplicity
No covering geometry is claimed to follow from Navier-Stokes dynamics
experiments.method: Define the logarithmic Hausdorff-content ledger of a ball cover, aggregate the local commutator bounds by distribution functions, and propagate the resulting coefficient through quadratic absorption, interpolation, Young, damping, and the vorticity-tail estimate.
experiments.certificate: A written covering lemma with a sharpness example and exact exponent checks for equal-radius and polynomial-radius covers.
experiments.success_criterion: The proof identifies the exact cover cost, the weakest uniform vanishing condition for absorption, and the critical equal-radius core-count threshold.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The proposed entropy does not control the restricted weak-L(3/2) norm or its energy-step exponent bookkeeping is incorrect.

experiments.title: Zero-set obstruction to logarithmic-BMO vorticity direction
experiments.hypothesis: Smooth divergence-free vorticity need not admit a normalized direction with the bmo_(1/|log r|) modulus across its zero set.
invariants: The vorticity is smooth, compactly supported, and divergence-free
Changing values on the measure-zero zero axis cannot change mean oscillation
experiments.method: Take the curl of a compactly supported vector potential that yields (x1,-x2,0) near the origin and compute its scale-invariant directional oscillation.
experiments.certificate: The symmetry calculation gives mean zero and mean oscillation exactly one on every centered small ball.
experiments.success_criterion: The weighted-BMO seminorm diverges for every assignment on the zero axis.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The local direction average or divergence-free construction is incorrect.

experiments.title: Exact Biot-Savart unidirectional strain contraction
experiments.hypothesis: The unidirectional Biot-Savart contribution has zero Rayleigh contraction along the fixed vorticity direction, although its strain tensor need not vanish.
invariants: Levi-Civita convention epsilon_123 equals 1
The human index derivation is the proof and the finite grid is only a check
experiments.method: Differentiate Biot-Savart in indices, symmetrize, contract, and check the finite-dimensional tensor identity with exact integers.
experiments.certificate: A written principal-value derivation, a nonzero-tensor counterexample, and exact unit tests.
experiments.success_criterion: The Rayleigh numerator equals the scalar triple-product form and vanishes for every unidirectional source tested.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The tensor signs, index contractions, or stated cancellation are wrong.

experiments.title: Localized 2607 commutator reconstruction
experiments.hypothesis: The localized logarithmic commutator estimate at source L232-371 survives exact BMO extension, weak-Lorentz interpolation, and far-field reconstruction.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Every operator is treated componentwise
No constant depends on radius or time
No downstream theorem conclusion is assumed
experiments.method: Match the Jones domain seminorm and ball geometry, interpolate one fixed CRW commutator from L(4/3) and L2, and recompute the exact kernel rearrangement and truncated dyadic shells.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored reconstruction with a constant ledger and explicit repairs.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-05 through O2607-07 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The preprint's localized logarithmic smoothing theorem is not available in its stated form.

experiments.title: Truncated 2607 vorticity-energy reconstruction
experiments.hypothesis: The source's positive-level vorticity test, Lorentz interpolation, Young absorption, damping, and terminal-interval threshold yield the claimed logarithmically improved high-vorticity tail.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Testing is justified at vorticity zeros
One threshold is uniform on the stated terminal interval
No downstream rearrangement claim is assumed
experiments.method: Apply Kato regularisation, justify the positive-level test by space-time approximation, verify each Lorentz and Sobolev exponent, solve the damped scalar inequality, and retain explicit high-level cutoffs.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored energy derivation and constant ledger.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-08 through O2607-10 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The claimed logarithmic vorticity distribution gain is not established from the repaired localized commutator estimate.

experiments.title: Logarithmic 2607 rearrangement and velocity transfer
experiments.hypothesis: The repaired high-vorticity distribution tail can be inverted, transferred through the whole-space Biot-Savart kernel by O'Neil's theorem, and inverted again without losing its logarithmic exponent.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
Both inversions retain explicit cutoffs and inequality directions
The O'Neil integral is controlled on the whole volume axis
Velocity is distinguished from its Biot-Savart representative
experiments.method: Use explicit trial level and trial volume functions, compute the exact Riesz-kernel rearrangement, bound both O'Neil terms, and isolate the harmonic-background normalisation.
experiments.certificate: The written source-anchored inversion and convolution derivation with cutoff and normalisation ledgers.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-11 and O2607-12 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The logarithmic gain does not rigorously transfer from vorticity distribution to the absolute velocity distribution used by the endgame.

experiments.title: Sparse-analyticity and harmonic-measure endgame
experiments.hypothesis: The repaired velocity distribution tail can be synchronized with local spatial analyticity and a correctly posed slit-domain harmonic-measure argument to exclude the proposed first singular time.
invariants: Pinned arXiv:2607.08866v2 TeX
The evaluation time remains strictly below T-star
One sparse radius works around every spatial centre
Velocity harmonic background is explicitly normalized or uniformly bounded
The final contradiction occurs only after taking the spatial supremum
experiments.method: Choose an explicit sparse radius from the velocity tail, prove same-scale 3D-to-1D sparseness, evaluate at half a guaranteed local lifespan after an escape time, and apply the two-constants principle on the slit disk.
experiments.certificate: The source-anchored endgame reconstruction with exact geometric, temporal, harmonic-measure, and constant ledgers.
experiments.success_criterion: O2607-13 through O2607-16 each receive an independently checkable verified, repaired, or unsupported verdict and the exact scope of the conditional theorem is fixed.
experiments.failure_interpretation: The preprint's final regularity conclusion does not follow from the repaired upstream estimates.

experiments.title: Independent HWY profile and spectrum reproduction
experiments.hypothesis: The published residual and unstable-eigenvalue certificates can be regenerated independently.
invariants: Exact divergence-free basis
Directed rounding
Pinned source and code revisions
Independent implementation path
experiments.method: Rebuild approximate profile, finite-rank operator, quadrature bounds, and interval spectrum.
experiments.certificate: Machine-readable interval certificate checked by a small independent verifier.
experiments.success_criterion: All analytic bootstrap inequalities close using regenerated certified constants.
experiments.failure_interpretation: Classify the mismatch as implementation, source ambiguity, or theorem-critical gap.
